what_it_does: This file defines the central runtime configuration for the 'fabric' subsystem: shared storage path, OpenAI-compatible chat and embedding endpoints, default model/brain, timeouts, embedding dimension, a multi-layer embedding policy, and a hard prohibition on Gemini models.
observations: All values are environment-overridable with documented defaults.
STORE_DIR is a single shared path used by both the UI bridge and MCP faces.
DEFAULT_BASE_URL defaults to Ollama's local /v1 endpoint, with LiteLLM proxy as the intended but not default endpoint.
Embeddings use a separate endpoint (localhost:8007) and model (pplx-embed-context-v1-4b) from chat.
BGE-M3 at :8001 is described as dormant, while pplx is the live GPU-served embedder.
Timeouts are split into DEFAULT_TIMEOUT and DEFAULT_CLOUD_TIMEOUT.
DEFAULT_EMB_DIM is set to 2560 to match the operative pplx embedder.
resolve_emb_layer maps a sentinel default and legacy aliases ('bge','bare') to concrete storage-layer tags.
forbid_gemini raises ValueError if a model name contains 'gemini'.
standouts: Hard, fail-loud ban on Gemini models.
Explicit multi-layer embedding resolution with a default layer and a reachable bare/legacy layer.
Embeddings endpoint is deliberately separate from the chat endpoint and not routed through LiteLLM.
conventions: Environment-variable-driven constants with uppercase names and inline default values.
Docstrings reference external design documents (AGENTS.md) and version markers (S6, D2).
Type hints on functions and explicit None return type.
Fail-loud validation via raised exceptions rather than silent fallback.
concerns: Operational status comments ('live', 'dormant') are date-stamped and may become stale.
DEFAULT_BASE_URL defaults to OLLAMA_DIRECT even though the comment says the LiteLLM proxy is the intended endpoint.
Embedding dimension is config-driven but not validated against the actual served model.
resolve_emb_layer collapses several distinct aliases ('bge','bare',None,'') to the same bare layer, which could surprise callers.
notes: This is the fabric runtime parameter surface: it centralizes paths, endpoints, model defaults, embedding-layer policy, and the Gemini prohibition.
questions: What do 'S6' and 'D2' specifically denote in this codebase?
How is the Gemini ban enforced across all callers that build model strings?
How does the storage layer know which embedding tags are valid beyond 'pplx' and the bare layer?
What validation occurs if COMPANY_EMB_DIM does not match the served model's output dimension?
Is there a mechanism to refresh or verify the live/dormant status of embedding endpoints?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it defines the unified endpoint, hard constraints, environment overrides, and embedding-layer resolution.
apparent_themes: single source of truth for shared store
intention_signals: The file tries to make fabric runtime behavior centrally configurable, keep the chat and embedding endpoints cleanly separated, allow reversible embedding-layer migrations, and enforce a non-negotiable ban on Gemini at the configuration boundary.
novelty: The embedding-layer resolution abstraction with a sentinel default and explicit legacy bare layer, plus operational live/dormant endpoint annotations embedded directly in configuration comments.
contribution: It contributes the canonical parameter surface for fabric: shared store path, endpoint URLs, default brain, timeout levers, embedding model/dimension, layer-resolution logic, and the forbidden-model guard function.
summary_for_embedding: fabric/config.py is the central environment-driven configuration module for the fabric subsystem, defining shared storage, chat and embedding endpoints, default models, timeouts, embedding dimensions, a multi-layer embedding policy, and a hard Gemini prohibition.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to consolidate fabric runtime constants and policy, keep chat and embedding endpoints separate, make embedding-layer selection explicit and reversible, and fail loudly on forbidden models.

symbols.does: Maps a caller's embedding-layer argument to a concrete storage-layer tag, applying configured defaults and legacy aliases.
symbols.description: resolve_emb_layer takes an emb argument and normalizes it: the sentinel EMB_LAYER_DEFAULT becomes DEFAULT_EMB_LAYER ('pplx' by default), while None, empty string, 'bge', or 'bare' collapse to None (the bare/legacy layer), and any other string is returned as an explicit layer tag. The function is idempotent on already-resolved values.

symbols.does: Raises ValueError if the supplied model name contains a forbidden Gemini identifier.
symbols.description: forbid_gemini checks the lowercased model string against the FORBIDDEN tuple (currently 'gemini') and raises a ValueError with a clear message if any forbidden substring is found, implementing the project's hard 'NO Gemini' policy.
